demographics.occupation: Barista / shift lead at a coffee chain

medications: Acetaminophen as needed for back pain (avoids NSAIDs due to CKD)
Melatonin 3 mg as needed for sleep
health.mental_health: Ongoing anxiety and low mood tied to an emotionally abusive partner; hypervigilant at home, trouble sleeping, and quietly planning a safe exit while trying to keep it hidden to avoid escalation.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for blood pressure and kidney labs (BMP/eGFR) last month
Urology visit for urinary frequency/nocturia and prostate medication adjustment about 2 months ago
Nephrology consult to review CKD staging and diet/hydration guidance about 4 months ago
Counseling intake at a community mental health clinic (kept private; scheduled around work) within the last 6 weeks

finance.debt: Carries about $6,500 on a credit card from car repairs and household expenses; partner pressures him to cover more bills and questions his spending, so he has started setting aside small amounts of cash and buying prepaid cards to prepare for leaving.
investments: Savings account at a credit union (kept low to avoid partner scrutiny)
recent_transactions: Prepaid debit card purchase (~$200) from a grocery store to build an emergency fund discreetly
Security deposit application fee for a senior/55+ apartment waitlist in Minneapolis
Money order for a portion of rent/utilities to avoid shared online banking access

relationships: Partner: Marisol Vega (emotionally abusive; monitors his phone and criticizes his work hours; he is planning to leave quietly)
Adult daughter: Elena Castillo (lives in St. Paul; supportive but he limits what he shares to avoid involving her in conflict)
Coworker confidant: Jada Nguyen (assistant manager; aware something is wrong at home and helps with shift swaps)
Older brother: Tomas Castillo (lives in Duluth; occasional check-ins, offers a spare room if needed)
social.social_media_activity: Low activity; mostly reads local news and coffee groups on Facebook. Rarely posts photos. Uses privacy settings tightly and has begun using a separate email and a new Google Voice number for housing and counseling contacts.
private_conversations: Asking his daughter how to leave safely without his partner finding out
Discussing emotional abuse patterns and safety planning with a counselor
Quietly coordinating a temporary place to stay with his brother
Talking with a coworker about changing availability so he can attend appointments without questions at home

schedule.work_hours: Typically 32–38 hours/week. Rotating shifts: opens (5:00–1:00) three days/week and mids (9:00–5:00) two days/week; covers occasional closing shift when short-staffed.
regular_appointments: Monthly lab work for kidney function and blood pressure check (early morning, before opening shifts when possible)
Urology follow-up every 6 months (more frequent if symptoms flare)
Biweekly counseling sessions (scheduled on weekdays he has mid shifts or days off)
upcoming_events: Tour of a 55+ apartment building in Northeast Minneapolis (kept off shared calendar)
HR meeting about schedule stability and staffing coverage next week
Kidney labs due in two weeks
schedule.daily_routine: Up early, coffee and light breakfast, bus or drives to the store; runs the morning floor, does cash counts and breaks coverage. After work he often stops at the library or a quiet café to make calls and look at housing listings before going home to avoid conflict.

professional.role: Barista / shift lead at a coffee chain
projects: Training two new baristas on opening procedures and drink standards
Reducing waste by tracking pastry pull and end-of-day discard
Updating the shift checklist for cash handling and safe counts
Improving drive-thru times during morning rush with a new staging routine
professional.performance_issues: Generally reliable, but stress at home has led to occasional distracted moments (missed a temperature log once, nearly shorted a safe count). He has requested more consistent shifts for medical appointments without explaining the home situation.
workplace_relationships: Supportive assistant manager (Jada Nguyen) who helps with discreet schedule adjustments
Tension with a newer shift lead who thinks Andrei is 'too slow' during peak, despite his strong customer service
Good rapport with regulars, including a retired nurse who notices his fatigue and asks after his health

hobbies: Walking around Lake Nokomis and the Midtown Greenway
Cooking simple soups and rice-and-vegetable meals (renal-friendly tweaks)
Secondhand book browsing and using the Minneapolis Central Library
Listening to public radio and classic rock playlists
Small home coffee experiments (pour-over, dialing in grind) when he has the energy
habits: Tracks blood pressure at a pharmacy kiosk and writes it in a small notebook
Avoids NSAIDs and limits salt/protein portions due to kidney guidance
Keeps a 'go bag' hidden with copies of ID, meds list, and a spare set of keys
Uses incognito browsing and clears call logs because his partner checks his phone
Occasional late-night snacking when anxious, then regrets it due to blood pressure
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives in a shared apartment in south Minneapolis with his partner; the home environment is tense and unpredictable, so he spends extra time in public places after work and is quietly preparing to move out without confrontation.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Prefers low-sodium meals, moderate protein, and lots of vegetables; limits cola and energy drinks. Drinks coffee daily at work but tries to cut off caffeine by early afternoon to help sleep and urinary symptoms.
